supply_chain.dependencies: Approximately 96% of products produced outside the U.S., with approximately 20% sourced from Vietnam, 16% from Cambodia, and 12% from China. Company does not own or operate any manufacturing facilities and depends exclusively on independent third parties for manufacture.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions from capacity constraints, closed factories, labor shortages, pandemic diseases, labor disputes, strikes, port congestion, scrutiny or detention of goods from certain territories (UFLPA, CAATSA), shipping delays from Red Sea crisis, disruptions to Suez and Panama canals, inflationary pressures, scarcity of raw materials, man-made or natural disasters, severe weather, military conflicts, terrorist acts

regulatory_risks: Imposition of additional tariffs, duties, taxes on imports or exports
Changes to existing trade agreements
Sanctions, restrictions, and other responses from trade policy changes
Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act (UFLPA) - detention, exclusion, or seizure of goods and monetary penalties
Countering America's Adversaries Through Sanctions Act (CAATSA)
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
U.K. Bribery Act compliance
Product labeling and product safety requirements
Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act
California Transparency in Supply Chains Act
Dodd-Frank conflict minerals disclosure requirements
Environmental regulations and climate change legislation
Data privacy and security regulations (including EU regulations)
Labor laws and regulations (minimum wage, overtime, working conditions, citizenship requirements)
Tax law changes (including OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax)
Import and export regulations
Anti-dumping and countervailing duties
Trademark and intellectual property protection laws (varying by jurisdiction)
ESG-related disclosure requirements
State and local regulations prohibiting or limiting ESG considerations

sanction_risks: Russia-Ukraine war impact on operations and broader macroeconomic implications
Israel-Hamas war and other Middle East hostilities
Militant attacks on cargo vessels in the Red Sea
Potential sanctions on countries from which products are sourced
UFLPA and CAATSA sanctions on goods from certain territories
U.S. tariff increases on imported goods and retaliatory tariffs from other countries
Diplomatic tensions between U.S. and other countries
Anti-American sentiment from changes in international trade relations
